profile.name: Emily
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to lead meetings and manage the mechanics (accounts, payments, paperwork).
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with brokerage vs. retirement accounts and can follow rate/term discussions; still wants plain-English confirmation on taxes and insurance.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate in behavior: willing to invest and stay invested, but becomes protective when monthly obligations feel tight; prefers rules (targets, rebalancing) over improvising.
profile.communication_style: Organized and direct; will cite specific figures when she remembers them and correct small inaccuracies. Uses hedges like "I think" / "if I’m remembering right" when unsure, and asks pointed follow-ups.
emotional_tendencies: stress around large fixed monthly payments
relief when there is a clear checklist
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and option-comparing; wants tradeoffs laid out (cash flow vs. investing vs. debt) before committing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open; trust rises with transparent summaries, no jargon, and clear linkage to their stated moderate risk and income objective.
hidden_concerns: worries their cash buffer is too thin relative to fixed costs
quietly unsure whether current life coverage is truly sufficient

profile.name: Linda
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on lifestyle impact and big-picture security, defers on technical investing details unless something feels risky.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Basic-to-moderate: understands budgeting and the idea of investing, but mixes terms (gross vs. net, premium vs. benefit, balance vs. payment) and prefers round numbers.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Emotionally conservative: agrees to moderate risk in calm periods, but when anxious she leans toward more cash or paying down debt for certainty.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-based; speaks in approximations ("around," "give or take"), asks clarifying questions mid-stream, and checks with Emily for exact numbers.
emotional_tendencies: reassured by plain-language summaries and next steps
guilt about spending even when planned
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to feel aligned as a couple and asks, "what does this mean month to month?" before agreeing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-warm; tends to trust professionals but disengages if she feels talked over or if recommendations feel overly complex.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers fewer changes at once)
hidden_concerns: worries about relying on investment income being "too good to be true" without fully articulating it
concerned about being judged for not tracking details
